channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: OpenAI executive shuffle includes new role for COO Brad Lightcap to lead ‘special projects’
item.description: In addition to Lightcap's new role, OpenAI CMO Kate Rouch will be stepping away from the company to focus on cancer recovery, with a plan to return when her health allows.

item.title: 'It embarrasses the 9700X': Reviewer raves about one of Intel's best CPU releases in years as Core Ultra 200S series pummels similarly priced AMD rivals
item.description: Intel’s Core Ultra 270K Plus delivers impressive rendering, editing, and AI performance, often outperforming older Intel and similarly priced AMD CPUs.

item.title: Anthropic buys biotech startup Coefficient Bio in $400M deal: reports
item.description: Anthropic has purchased the stealth biotech AI startup Coefficient Bio in a $400 million stock deal, according to The Information and Eric Newcomer.

item.title: OpenAI’s AGI boss is taking a leave of absence
item.description: OpenAI is undergoing another round of C-suite changes, according to an internal memo viewed by The Verge. Fidji Simo, OpenAI's CEO of AGI deployment - who was until recently the company's CEO of Applications - says in the memo that she will be stepping away on medical leave "for the next several weeks" due to […]

item.title: Anthropic ramps up its political activities with a new PAC
item.description: With the midterms right around the corner, the new group is positioned to back candidates who support the AI company's policy agenda.

item.title: Jason Blundell announces his new-new studio, Magic Fractal
item.description: Jason Blundell, a former creative lead on Activision Blizzard's Call of Duty: Black Ops series, has announced that he's formed a new development studio called Magic Fractal Studios. Blundell announced the studio on a livestream with former Dark Outlaw Games coworker JC Farmer, a little over a week after the previous studio was closed by Sony.Little is known about Magic Fractal beyond its logo and social media accounts. Dark Outlaw Games was similarly mysterious, and closed before it could even announce its first project. All that's known is what Blundell has shared after the studio was shut do...

item.title: US Airlines Are Hiking Bag Fees Amid Iran War Oil Crisis
item.description: United Airlines and JetBlue announced this week that they are raising baggage fees.

item.title: Our Favorite iPad Is $50 Off
item.description: Score an 11th-generation A16-powered iPad for just $300.

item.title: This Ridiculously Simple Trick Might Stop Gulls From Nabbing Your Lunch
item.description: Googly eyes are apparently one of a gull's biggest fears.

item.title: Banks working on the SpaceX IPO reportedly have to subscribe to Grok
item.description: Cutthroat capitalism sure does bring out the best in humanity. Take, for example, Elon Musk. He reportedly set a condition for banks, law firms, auditors and advisors who want to work on the SpaceX IPO. They're required to subscribe to the bastion of nonconsensual deepfakes and occasional Hitler praise known as Grok.Surely the best and brightest banks our nation has to offer would refuse such a pay-to-play demand, right? Well, no. The New York Times reports that some of them have agreed to spend tens of millions of dollars on Grok. Those financial institutions are said to already be integratin...

item.title: ‘Starfleet Academy’ Season 3 Could Have Done the ‘Voyager’ Sequel Episode of Robert Picardo’s Dreams
item.description: According to the 'Voyager' star, he pitched a sequel to the classic 'Living Witness' that we'll now never see.

item.title: Best iPad apps to boost productivity and make your life easier
item.description: There are many iPad apps to help you organize recipes, sync tasks across devices, be more productive, and manage your notes.

item.title: AI companies are building huge natural gas plants to power data centers. What could go wrong?
item.description: Meta, Microsoft, and Google are all betting big on new natural gas power plants to run their AI data centers. They may regret it.

item.title: I turned the Artemis II mission's most stunning Earth photo into an iPhone wallpaper — but I needed a little help from AI
item.description: Commander Reid Wiseman's unforgettable image of Earth is now part of my iPhone 17 Pro Max

item.title: OpenAI’s Fidji Simo Is Taking Medical Leave Amid an Executive Shake-Up
item.description: The company is undergoing major leadership restructuring as its CEO of AGI deployment goes on leave for “several weeks.”

item.title: Edward ‘Big Balls’ Coristine Is Helping Out on Viral Fraud Videos Now
item.description: The DOGE engineer appeared on influencer Nick Shirley's podcast this week to talk corruption and government work—and how they collaborated on Shirley's recent ‘investigation.’

item.title: Judge Kills Lawsuit Against Infamous Study That Might Have Increased Teen Suicides
item.description: A long-criticized clinical trial claiming that the antidepressant Paxil is safe for teens had already led to a $3 billion settlement and a federal guilty plea in 2012.

item.title: Save over $500 on this powerful Alienware 16X Aurora gaming laptop with an RTX 5060
item.description: Dell's spring sale is live, and I'm highlighting this powerful Alienware 16X Aurora gaming laptop that's on sale for an incredible price of $1,949.99.

item.title: People would rather have an Amazon warehouse in their backyard than a data center
item.description: A new poll shows that the debate over data centers is far from settled.

item.title: Lenovo Legion Go 2 suddenly costs $650 more as RAMageddon lays waste to gaming hardware
item.description: Remember when we thought the Legion Go 2 was expensive at $1,099 and up? Those were the days - Best Buy is now listing Lenovo's handheld for $1,499 with a Ryzen Z2 or $1,999 with a Z2 Extreme. The latter originally cost $1,349, so that's a $650 jump in just six months. And yes, that […]

item.title: Researchers Discover How to Add Psilocybin, DMT, and Other Psychedelics to Tobacco
item.description: AI assisted with the study, which could make it cheaper and easier to produce these mind-bending drugs.

item.title: HarperCollins is forging ahead with AI-assisted dramas based on books. Some authors have concerns
item.description: Reactions to two announcements this week underscore how book publishers are treading into thorny new territory. HarperCollins Publishers and AI-powered animation studio Toonstar have announced a multi-year partnership to co-produce original YouTube series based on HarperCollins titles.

item.title: Fake Videos Are Already Spreading After Two US Air Force Planes Go Down Near Iran
item.description: Fake images are everywhere when big news breaks.

item.title: We’re Obsessed With the Meta Fits on These New Star Wars Toys
item.description: You didn't know you needed to see Stormtroopers and Battle Droids in Star Wars t-shirts but you do. You really do.

item.title: IKEA just launched the perfect balcony gardening starter kit — 12 essentials from $1.99
item.description: Don't let a lack of space quash your gardening dreams.

item.title: One of the most infamous seafood promotions in history could be making a comeback. But there’s a catch
item.description: Not everyone is convinced that endless shrimp killed an entire restaurant chain, but rumored modifications indicate that it learned from past mistakes. Red Lobster might be taking an old page from its playbook to win over consumers’ hearts.

item.title: United just made your suitcase more expensive, Here’s how much
item.description: A new fee hike could push even more travelers to skip checked bags, setting up a messy summer in the skies. The battle for overhead bin space on flights is likely to intensify as United Airlines announced it will increase the checked bag fees starting Friday. It will now cost $10 more to check luggage for passengers traveling on flights in the U.S., Mexico, Canada, and Latin America. ...

item.title: Olivia Rodrigo’s new album ditches her iconic brand. Is it a marketing masterclass or a misstep?
item.description: The singer’s latest record abandons her pattern of four-letter titles and signature purple color. Fans aren’t sure what to think. With her first two albums, Olivia Rodrigo established a pattern. Her signature color? Purple, which served as the backdrop for both covers. Her naming convention? Four-letter words, stylized in all-caps: SOUR for her 2021 debut and GUTS for her 2023 follow-up. ...
